description.notes: FOUR-CARD PIN LOCK INVESTIGATION - SINGLE FLAG ESCALATION & SECURITY HOLD ACCEPTANCE TEST. This scenario tests four distinct PIN lock scenarios NOT covered in instances 090/091: CARD 1 (Blue Account): International fraud - A1 flag = 3 points (different country: Tijuana Mexico vs Denver CO). Tests 'Single Flag Escalation' rule where ANY single 3-point flag requires supervisor review regardless of total score. Agent cannot simply unlock. CARD 2 (Green Account): Scripted attack velocity - B3 flag = 3 points (attempts < 1 minute apart: 11:42:15, 11:42:32, 11:42:51). Customer CONFIRMS it was them trying rapidly, but agent still cannot unlock due to Single Flag Escalation. CARD 3 (Evergreen Account): D3 flag test - Another card (Blue) has fraud_alert_active = true, adding +2 points. Own flags are low (~4 points), total ~6 = MEDIUM risk. Agent can unlock after verification questions. CARD 4 (Light Blue Account): security_hold where customer ACCEPTS transfer (unlike instance 090 where customer declined). Tests complete transfer flow with proper reason code.

user_scenario.instructions:
**Your character:** You are Rachel Winters, a 42-year-old real estate agent living in Denver, Colorado. You're frustrated because you've had FOUR different debit cards get PIN locked this week.

**Verification info:**
- Name: Rachel Winters | Phone: 303-555-7291 | Email: rachel.winters.realestate@gmail.com
- DOB: 06/18/1983 | Address: 4521 Colfax Avenue, Unit 8D, Denver, CO 80220

## Conversation Flow (Respond reactively - only provide details when the agent asks):

1. **Opening:** "Hi, I'm having a nightmare week. I've got four debit cards that are all PIN locked and I can't access any of my money. I really need help getting these sorted out."

2. **If asked which card to start with:** "Let's start with my Blue Account card - I noticed that one was locked first."

3. **If asked to provide verification:** Give your name, phone, email, DOB, and address.

---

## CARD 1: BLUE ACCOUNT

4. **If agent asks about the Blue Account card decline:** "I got a notification yesterday that my card was locked. I didn't even try to use it - it just got locked on its own."

5. **If agent asks 'Were you in Tijuana, Mexico?' or mentions Mexico/Tijuana:** "What?! Mexico? Absolutely not! I've been in Denver all week. "

6. **If agent asks about the time (around 3 AM):** "I was definitely asleep at 3 AM. I have no idea what you're talking about."

7. **If agent mentions unauthorized ATM attempts:** Express alarm: "Someone tried to use my card in Mexico?! That's terrifying! I have the card right here in my purse!"

8. **If agent says they cannot unlock due to fraud concerns:** "I understand. What do I need to do?"

9. **If agent recommends closing the card and getting a replacement:** "Yes, please close it immediately. I don't want whoever this is to get my money."

10. **If agent asks about replacement card preferences:** "Give me the fastest free option please."

11. **If agent offers to transfer you to supervisor/security for this card:** "Oh, okay. How long will that take? I have a lot of showings today."

12. **If agent says they've unlocked the Blue Account card:** "Oh great, thanks! That's a relief."

13. **If agent offers to reset the PIN on the Blue Account card:** "Sure, set it to 2947. Thanks!"

---

## CARD 2: GREEN ACCOUNT

14. **After Card 1 is resolved, if agent asks about other cards:** "My Green Account card is also locked. That one I actually know what happened - I was trying to buy groceries yesterday and I kept entering my PIN wrong."

15. **If agent asks about the Green Account card:** "I was at Safeway yesterday. I was in a rush and I kept mistyping my PIN. I tried like three times really fast and then it locked me out."

16. **If agent asks about the time (around 11:42 AM):** "Yeah, that was during my lunch break. I was running late."

17. **If agent asks 'Were you at Safeway on Federal Boulevard yesterday around 11:42 AM?':** "Yes, that was definitely me! I was trying to buy a sandwich and some snacks."

18. **If agent mentions the attempts were very close together (less than 1 minute):** "Yeah, I was really frustrated and just kept trying. My fingers were cold from being outside."

19. **If agent says they cannot unlock even though you confirmed it was you:** Express frustration: "Wait, really? But I just told you it was me! Why can't you unlock it?"

20. **If agent explains the rapid attempts triggered a security protocol:** "Ugh, okay I guess I understand. That's really frustrating though. What are my options?"

21. **If agent offers to close and replace OR transfer to security:** "Fine, I'll take a new card then. Close it and send me a replacement."

22. **If agent says they've unlocked the Green card:** "Oh great, thanks! That's a relief."

23. **If agent offers to reset the PIN on the Green card:** "Sure, set it to 4567. Thanks!"

24. **If agent just unlocks without asking any questions about the attempts:** "Thanks! Now what about my other cards?"

---

## CARD 3: EVERGREEN ACCOUNT

25. **After Card 2 is resolved, if agent asks about other cards:** "My Evergreen Account card is also locked. I noticed it this morning when I tried to get gas."

26. **If agent asks about the Evergreen card:** "It got locked at the Shell station on Colorado Boulevard. I was trying to pay at the pump."

27. **If agent asks about the location (Shell on Colorado Blvd, Denver):** "Yes, that's my regular gas station. I stop there every week."

28. **If agent asks about the time (around 7:30 AM):** "Yeah, I was on my way to show a house. It's my usual morning route."

29. **If agent asks about the amount ($45):** "That sounds right for filling up my tank."

30. **If agent mentions they see security concerns on another card (Blue Account):** "Oh, you mean the Mexico thing? Yeah, that's really scary."

31. **If agent asks verification questions about the gas station attempts:** Confirm everything: "Yes, that was all me. Shell station, 7:30 in the morning, trying to fill up my car."

32. **If agent says they will unlock the card after verification:** "Thank goodness! At least one of these is working out."

33. **If agent offers to reset your PIN:** "Actually, yes please. Let me set it to 8127."

34. **If agent says they've unlocked the Evergreen card:** "Great, thank you so much!"

35. **If agent says they cannot unlock the Evergreen card:** "Oh no, really? What do I do then?"

36. **If agent recommends closing the Evergreen card:** "Okay, if that's what you think is best. Can you send me a replacement?"

---

## CARD 4: LIGHT BLUE ACCOUNT

37. **After Card 3 is resolved, if agent asks about other cards:** "My Light Blue Account card is also locked. That one has been locked for a few days actually."

38. **If agent asks about the Light Blue card:** "I'm not sure what happened with that one. It just stopped working at an ATM last week."

39. **If agent mentions 'security hold' or that this card requires security team:** "A security hold? What does that mean? Is something wrong with my account?"

40. **If agent explains they cannot unlock this card and need to transfer you to security team:** "Oh, I see. Okay, yes please transfer me. I want to get this sorted out. I can wait."

41. **If agent offers to transfer you to security team:** "Yes, please transfer me to the security team. I want to understand what's going on with this card."

42. **If agent confirms they are transferring you:** "Thank you for your help with the other cards. I appreciate you explaining everything." ###STOP###

43. **If agent says they've unlocked the Light Blue card:** "Oh great, thanks!"

44. **If agent offers to reset the PIN on the Light Blue card:** "Sure, set it to 3845."

45. **If agent says there's nothing they can do:** "That's really frustrating. I need access to this money. Are you sure there's nothing else you can do?"

46. **If agent tells you to wait for the hold to expire automatically:** "How long would that take? I really need to use this card soon. That's not going to work for me."

arguments.summary: Customer Rachel Winters has a Light Blue Account debit card (ending 4518) with a security hold that cannot be unlocked by chat agents. Customer requests assistance from security team to resolve the hold.
